goal_spec_json.goal_statement: Sleep a minimum of 6 hours consistently every night

morning_briefing.morning_message:
Morning Ameena.

You said you feel like a perfect 100 this morning — that's wonderful to hear. Let's look at what your body is telling us. Last night you got 6.6 hours of sleep, which is right around your usual, but your sleep efficiency was down at 88.7% with 11.3% of the night spent awake — that's more fragmented than normal. Yesterday's sleep quality rating was a 71, reflecting how you felt about the night before.

Here's the deeper story: yesterday you were incredibly active — 16k steps and 92 minutes of MVPA — but your physiological recovery signals collapsed. Your HRV hit zero, and your internal recovery score dropped to a 2/100. That's a massive mismatch: your body is screaming for rest while your mind and behavior are pushing hard. Your subjective energy was critically low at 37, even though your focus remained sky-high at 91. The late eating (last meal at 10:39 PM) likely didn't help that sleep fragmentation either.

Today isn't about adding more output; it's about honoring that recovery gap. Your body needs gentle signals to restore itself, not more strain.

Your actions today are all about giving your system the repair space it's asking for, without adding pressure.

Let today be the day you listen to what your physiology has been shouting.

assumptions: User can adjust evening schedule for earlier eating
User has access to protein-rich foods
User can take short breaks during work
User has phone/alarm device for external cues
Nutrition confidence (0.62) suggests estimates may be approximate
soft_constraints: caution with high energy expenditure given recent calorie/protein deficits
consider gentle activity to counter sedentary trend
leverage high energy/focus for sleep routine implementation

generated_actions.title: After your morning coffee, take 5 minutes of gentle stretching or mobility work
generated_actions.rationale: Physiological recovery signals are critically depressed: HRV was zero yesterday (-92.39 fast z-score) and physio proxy score is 2 (-47.37 fast z-score), sharply declining. Despite your high self-reported energy (100), this indicates severe internal strain. Gentle movement after a routine activity (morning coffee) promotes parasympathetic activation without taxing your system, directly addressing the 'low_recovery' risk flag and 'high_activity_with_critical_recovery_mismatch' cross-domain signal.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you complete 5 minutes of gentle stretching after your morning coffee?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports completing at least 5 minutes of light stretching/mobility after coffee

assumptions: You have space to move
You can spare 5 minutes after coffee
generated_actions.description: Right after you finish your morning coffee, spend 5 minutes doing gentle stretches or mobility exercises like leg swings, shoulder rolls, and torso twists. Keep it light, no heavy exertion.

generated_actions.title: Set a 9:00 PM 'last snack' alarm and finish eating by 7:30 PM
generated_actions.rationale: Yesterday's last meal was at 22:39 (1359 minutes) and eating window was 859 minutes (~14.3 hours), trending later (+11.69 slope). This aligns with elevated sleep wake ratio (11.26%) and reduced efficiency (88.74%). The 'late_nutrition_with_sleep_fragmentation' cross-domain signal suggests timing mismatch may contribute to sleep fragmentation. This directly addresses the 'nutrition_gap' and 'sleep_debt' risk flags and your sleep goal domain.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did your 9:00 PM alarm help you stop eating and start winding down earlier?
evaluation.success_definition: Last meal occurs before 7:30 PM (time_last_meal_minutes < 1050) AND user reports the alarm was helpful

assumptions: You have a phone or alarm device
You can adjust your evening schedule
generated_actions.description: Set an alarm for 9:00 PM tonight. When it rings, that's your cue to stop any eating and begin winding down. Aim to actually finish dinner by 7:30 PM local time.

must_avoid: continuing screen use after the alarm

generated_actions.title: When you feel tension building in the afternoon, pause for 3 rounds of 4-7-8 breathing
generated_actions.rationale: Stress score was elevated yesterday (35) and rising (+1.31 slope), while workload perception is high (71) and also rising (+5.12 slope). With tight schedule and high workload hard constraints, this micro-intervention uses implementation intention ('when you feel tension') to disrupt stress accumulation, supporting the 'stress_coping' domain status and leveraging your high focus (91) for intentional regulation.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you use the 4-7-8 breathing technique when you felt tension today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports using the breathing technique at least once when stress arose

assumptions: You can take brief pauses during work
generated_actions.description: If you notice stress or tension rising during your high-workload afternoon, immediately pause and do 3 rounds of 4-7-8 breathing: inhale for 4 seconds, hold for 7, exhale for 8.

generated_actions.title: After brushing your teeth tonight, dim lights and avoid screens for 15 minutes
generated_actions.rationale: Sleep efficiency was below baseline yesterday (88.74, -3.17 fast z-score) and declining (-1.06 slope), with elevated wake ratio (11.26%, +1.06 slope). Habit-stacking to your existing bedtime routine (brushing teeth) creates a consistent wind-down to improve sleep continuity, directly addressing the 'sleep_debt' risk flag and supporting your sleep goal domain.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you spend 15 minutes screen-free with dim lights after brushing teeth?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports completing at least 15 minutes of screen-free wind-down with dim lighting

assumptions: You have access to dim lighting
You can avoid screens for 15 minutes
generated_actions.description: Immediately after brushing your teeth before bed, dim the lights in your bedroom and avoid phones/computers for 15 minutes. You can read a book, listen to calm music, or do light tidying.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: After your morning coffee, plan a protein-rich lunch
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Nutrition status is below_avg: yesterday's calorie (1297 kcal) and protein (65g) intakes were substantially below baselines and trending sharply downward (-184 kcal, -5.9g protein slopes). This creates a 'calorie_deficit_amid_high_energy' cross-domain risk. Habit-stacking lunch planning to your morning coffee routine directly addresses the 'nutrition_gap' risk flag and the soft constraint 'caution with high energy expenditure given recent calorie/protein deficits'. Using yesterday's actual meal ('Breaded chicken salad') as a reference reinforces a positive pattern.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did planning your lunch after coffee help you eat a protein-rich meal earlier?
evaluation.success_definition: Daily protein intake reaches at least 70g (addressing the sharp decline) AND user reports the planning was helpful

assumptions: You have access to protein-rich foods
You can plan your lunch
previous_day_generated_actions.description: When you finish your morning coffee, decide on a lunch that includes a substantial protein source (like grilled chicken, fish, or tofu) to be eaten before 2:00 PM. Yesterday's lunch was 'Breaded chicken and mixed lettuce salad' – keep that protein focus but aim for earlier timing.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: Set a 2:00 PM 'caffeine cutoff' alarm on your phone
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Yesterday's caffeine intake was late (iced latte at 23:50), contributing to the 'late_stimulants_with_early_sleep_onset' cross-domain signal. This dissociation between late stimulants and excellent early sleep onset (1 minute) suggests potential future sleep disruption if the pattern continues. This external cue directly addresses the 'late_caffeine' risk flag and supports your sleep goal domain and today's 'sleep_consistency_recovery' priority. It varies from recent '2:00 PM cutoff' actions by specifying the phone alarm as a daily recurring cue.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did your 2:00 PM alarm help you stop caffeine intake on time?
evaluation.success_definition: No caffeine is logged after 2:00 PM today AND user reports the alarm was helpful

assumptions: You have a phone or alarm device
previous_day_generated_actions.description: Set a daily recurring alarm for 2:00 PM. When it rings, that's your signal to have your last caffeinated drink for the day. Yesterday you had an iced latte at 23:50 (64mg caffeine) – keeping caffeine to the morning today protects tonight's sleep.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: When you finish a major work block, take a 5-minute walk or stretch break
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Despite exceptionally high activity yesterday (19k steps, 105 MVPA minutes), sedentary time was 676 minutes and focus was high (93). The soft constraint 'consider gentle activity to counter sedentary trend' and hard constraints 'tight schedule' and 'high workload' make a short, intentional break ideal. This uses implementation intention ('when you finish a major work block') to make it automatic and varies from recent 'standing/breathing break' actions by emphasizing movement (walk or stretch) over breathing alone.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you take at least one 5-minute movement break after a work block today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports taking at least one movement break (walk or stretch) after prolonged focused work

assumptions: You can take short breaks during work
You have space to walk or stretch
previous_day_generated_actions.description: After you complete a significant task or 60-90 minutes of focused work, immediately stand up and either walk around for 5 minutes or do a full-body stretch sequence. Look away from your screen and move your body.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: Set a 9:00 PM 'eating cutoff' alarm to finish dinner by 7:30 PM
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Yesterday's last meal was very late (23:50, 1430 minutes) and eating window was long (860 minutes), trending later (+40.9 slope). This directly contradicts the soft constraint 'maintain earlier eating window (<1200 min)' and creates the 'late_stimulants_with_early_sleep_onset' cross-domain mismatch with your excellent early sleep onset. This action addresses the 'nutrition_timing' below_avg status and supports your sleep goal domain by creating a clear external boundary. It varies from recent 'last meal' alarms by specifying a 9:00 PM cue with a 7:30 PM target.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did your 9:00 PM alarm help you stop eating and start winding down earlier?
evaluation.success_definition: Last meal occurs before 7:30 PM (time_last_meal_minutes < 1050) AND user reports the alarm was helpful

assumptions: You have a phone or alarm device
You can adjust your evening schedule
previous_day_generated_actions.description: Set an alarm on your phone for 9:00 PM tonight. When it rings, that's your signal that any eating should be complete and to begin your wind-down. Aim to actually finish dinner by 7:30 PM local time.

must_avoid: continuing screen use after the alarm

previous_day_briefing.morning_message:
Morning Ameena! 

You're feeling great this morning with an 82/100 — and that high energy mode is absolutely backed up by your data. Last night was objectively excellent: 7 hours of solid sleep with 93% efficiency and you fell asleep almost instantly. That's your strongest sleep night recently, and it's fueling today's momentum perfectly.

Here's what's really interesting: despite that late iced latte and meal around midnight, your body still crashed into deep sleep immediately. That shows serious sleep resilience, but it's also a risky pattern — caffeine that late usually disrupts sleep quality even if you fall asleep fast. Yesterday you were on fire with activity (19k steps!) and your recovery metrics are soaring (HRV at 131 is fantastic), yet your nutrition intake was only 1,297 calories with protein trending sharply down. Your energy feels maxed out at 100/100, but running this active on that calorie deficit could catch up with you.

Today's about protecting what's working while adjusting the timing. You've got the sleep and recovery foundation nailed — now let's make sure your fueling and caffeine timing support that excellence. The theme is creating clear boundaries so your energy can stay this consistent.

With your activity tolerance this strong, today's about matching that intensity with intentional recovery windows.

evidence_quotes: I feel like the day was passing without me doing anything of value
Im finishing a load of laundry then ill head to bed and hopefully tomorrow is more productive

meal_descriptions.description: A cheese and vegetable omelet, a hashbrown, and a slice of zaatar manakeesh and 2 chicken sausages. And a mixed berry and granola yogurt cup.
A small banana and a protein bar
2 spoons of rice and chicken stew and a side salad.
Fresh mixed berry juice and date maamoul cookie
